title: gh-148222 Fix Null dereference bugs at genericaliasobject.c
author: prakashsellathurai
body:
<!--
Thanks for your contribution!
Please read this comment in its entirety. It's quite important.

# Pull Request title

It should be in the following format:

```
gh-NNNNNN: Summary of the changes made
```

Where: gh-NNNNNN refers to the GitHub issue number.

Most PRs will require an issue number. Trivial changes, like fixing a typo, do not need an issue.

# Backport Pull Request title

If this is a backport PR (PR made against branches other than `main`),
please ensure that the PR title is in the following format:

```
[X.Y] <title from the original PR> (GH-NNNNNN)
```

Where: [X.Y] is the branch name, for example: [3.13].

GH-NNNNNN refers to the PR number from `main`.

-->


<!-- gh-issue-number: gh-148222 -->
* Issue: gh-148222
<!-- /gh-issue-number -->

comments.author: prakashsellathurai
comments.body:
> Is it possible to write a test for the vectorcall part?
@serhiy-storchaka 

I spent some time on this.  since this bug occurs at C  implementations level, we cannot reproduce in python unit tests. 

also with custom  _testcapi extension  i am not ably to reliably simulate that exact flow

comments.author: prakashsellathurai
comments.body:
@serhiy-storchaka thanks for the pr review.

 all checks are passing. Since I don't have merge access, could you please merge this when you get a chance? Thank you!

comments.author: miss-islington-app[bot]
comments.body:
Thanks @prakashsellathurai for the PR, and @JelleZijlstra for merging it 🌮🎉.. I'm working now to backport this PR to: 3.13, 3.14.
🐍🍒⛏🤖

comments.author: bedevere-app[bot]
comments.body: [GH-148705](https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/148705) is a backport of this pull request to the [3.14 branch](https://github.com/python/cpython/tree/3.14).

comments.author: miss-islington-app[bot]
comments.body:
Sorry, @prakashsellathurai and @JelleZijlstra, I could not cleanly backport this to `3.13` due to a conflict.
Please backport using [cherry_picker](https://pypi.org/project/cherry-picker/) on command line.
```
cherry_picker 634568d030f18183212c01bd4544aa7f97e05442 3.13
```

comments.author: JelleZijlstra
comments.body: Would you mind checking if the bug is also present on the 3.13 branch and backport it to 3.13 if so?
